name: Malik ibn Anas
title: Old Swordsman
shortDescription: An old and loyal companion of Imam Hussain (AS) who fought bravely and was martyred while reciting poems against the family of Abu Sufyan.
fullStory:
Malik ibn Anas al-Kahili was a brave old man and a sincere Shia of Kufa who reached Karbala with a deep love for the Prophet's (PBUH) family.

Despite his old age, he had a young heart and an iron will. On Ashura, when it was his turn to defend his Imam, he entered the battlefield with the Master of Martyrs' (AS) permission.

Malik recited very crushing epic poetry on the battlefield that is recorded in history. In his poems, he said: 'The family of Ali are the followers of the Merciful God, and the family of Abu Sufyan are the followers of Satan.'

Then, with a drawn sword, he attacked the army of disbelief and, despite his old age, displayed an astonishing battle. After killing a number of enemy soldiers, he finally attained the elevated rank of martyrdom and a blessed end.
quoteArabic: آلُ عَلِيٍّ شِيعَةُ الرَّحْمانِ، وَآلُ حَرْبٍ شِيعَةُ الشَّيْطانِ...
quote: (In his war poetry:) The family of Ali are the followers of the Merciful God, and the family of Abu Sufyan are the followers of Satan.
